item.author: @elonmusk

item.title:
I think this is a very interesting business case to discuss
Because as a business owner he's in the...
item.description:
I think this is a very interesting business case to discuss
Because as a business owner he's in the right, and legally he's got himself covered, he does state it's a subscription multiple times: - "ships free monthly" (although literally TINY in font-size:10px) - in the cart it states "delivers every 1 month" (font-size:13px) - in checkout "1 month subscription with 20% discount" (font-size:12px) - in checkout "recurring subtotal $48.00 every month" (font-size:14px) - in checkout "by continuing with your payment, you agree to the future charges listed on this page and the cancellation policy." (font-size:12px)
But as a customer I'd get angry here too, if I click through it fast while doing multiple things on a busy day (like most people), it's highly likely you miss it'a subscription because: - when you buy most things, they don't default to a subscription, you just buy a single thing, here it immediately defaults to a subscription - the single purchase option is shown below the CTA so it is possible: "One-Time Purchase - $180 $167 ($1.85 / Day)" but in TINY text again (font-size:14px) vs the subscription CTA that's in capitals "ADD TO CART" (font-size:16px)
I don't blame the guy either, B2C supplements businesses like these are brutal, getting someone to get a subscription means you can sell the same thing probably at least 3 times
But you're also counting on them to NOT see it's a subscription, and then secondly forgetting it's a subscription
So I'd call this 100% asshole design, but ironically very legally sound, I try keep the asshole design to a minimum on my sites, for Photo AI, I do have a it a little bit, I show a $349/year annual plan as "$29/mo billed annually $349" but I also have a self-refund if you did that wrong after
Anyway my point is it's not really black and white, competitors force you to do asshole design, or you make 3-4x less than them, meanwhile as a customer it feels you're being tricked
I'd always go with whatever design makes sense if you'd sell to your mom and you wouldn't want her to be tricked! Actually with most things in a business always ask what your mom would think! Would she approve or disapprove?
I call it "the mom test"
--- @levelsio | ❤️ 20 | 🔁 0 | 💬 3 | 👁 7120
item.author: @levelsio
